description: Seeded continuation of a real conversation: the prior turns (restored from the seed, not re-driven) already built 'Otter Digest' — a daily-9am Slack post of 'Daily digest ready' to #cosmic-otter-alerts, credential left for setup. The live turn asks for a schedule change. The agent must continue from the restored context: edit the existing workflow in place, without re-asking for established details and without building a new workflow.

conversation.text: Change the schedule to every 30 minutes instead.

buildExpectations: The agent modified the existing 'Otter Digest' workflow, rather than creating a new workflow or asking which workflow was meant.
The agent did not ask the user for any detail established earlier in the conversation (Slack channel, message text, workflow name or purpose).

After the live turn, the workflow's schedule trigger runs every 30 minutes, and the Slack step still posts 'Daily digest ready' to #cosmic-otter-alerts — the schedule change did not drop or alter the rest of the workflow.

executionScenarios.name: edited-schedule-fires
executionScenarios.description: The pre-existing Otter Digest workflow after the requested schedule change
executionScenarios.dataSetup: No special data needed: the schedule trigger fires once and the Slack node posts a single message. The Slack credential is intentionally unset (the user deferred setup).
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The schedule trigger is configured to run every 30 minutes (not daily at 9am). The Slack step posts the message 'Daily digest ready' to the #cosmic-otter-alerts channel. Judge the workflow's structure and parameters only — a Slack credential/auth failure at runtime is expected (the credential is deliberately unset) and must not fail the scenario.
